techniques.name: Drive-by Compromise
techniques.description: Adversaries may gain access to a system through a user visiting a website over the normal course of browsing.

techniques.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to exploit a weakness in an Internet-facing host or system to initially access a network.

techniques.name: External Remote Services
techniques.description: Adversaries may leverage external-facing remote services to initially access or persist within a network.

techniques.name: Hardware Additions
techniques.description: Adversaries may introduce computer accessories or networking hardware into a system that serves as a vector for access.

techniques.name: Phishing
techniques.description: Adversaries may send phishing messages to gain access to victim systems.

techniques.name: Replication Through Removable Media
techniques.description: Adversaries may move onto systems by copying malware to removable media and leveraging autorun features.

techniques.name: Supply Chain Compromise
techniques.description: Adversaries may manipulate products or product delivery mechanisms prior to receipt by a final consumer for data or system compromise.

sub_techniques.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools

techniques.name: Trusted Relationship
techniques.description: Adversaries may breach or leverage organizations with access to intended victims through trusted third-party relationships.

techniques.name: Valid Accounts
techniques.tactic: Defense Evasion, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, Initial Access
techniques.description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of existing accounts as a means of gaining initial access, persistence, privilege escalation, or defense evasion.

techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
techniques.description: Adversaries may abuse command and script interpreters to execute commands, scripts, or binaries.

techniques.name: Exploitation for Client Execution
techniques.description: Adversaries may exploit software vulnerabilities in client applications to execute code.

techniques.name: Inter-Process Communication
techniques.description: Adversaries may abuse inter-process communication mechanisms for local code or command execution.

techniques.name: Native API
techniques.description: Adversaries may interact with the native OS application programming interface to execute behaviors.

techniques.name: Scheduled Task/Job
techniques.description: Adversaries may abuse task scheduling functionality to facilitate initial or recurring execution of malicious code.

techniques.name: Shared Modules
techniques.description: Adversaries may execute malicious payloads via loading shared modules.

techniques.name: Software Deployment Tools
techniques.description: Adversaries may gain access to and use third-party software suites installed within an enterprise network to move laterally.

techniques.name: System Services
techniques.description: Adversaries may abuse system services or daemons to execute commands or programs.

techniques.name: User Execution
techniques.description: An adversary may rely upon specific actions by a user in order to gain execution.

techniques.name: Windows Management Instrumentation
techniques.description: Adversaries may abuse Windows Management Instrumentation to execute malicious commands and payloads.

techniques.name: Account Manipulation
techniques.description: Adversaries may manipulate accounts to maintain or elevate access to victim systems.

techniques.name: BITS Jobs
techniques.description: Adversaries may abuse BITS jobs to persistently execute code and perform various background tasks.

techniques.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution
techniques.description: Adversaries may configure system settings to automatically execute a program during system boot or logon.

techniques.name: Boot or Logon Initialization Scripts
techniques.description: Adversaries may use scripts automatically executed at boot or logon initialization to establish persistence.

techniques.name: Create Account
techniques.description: Adversaries may create an account to maintain access to victim systems.

techniques.name: Create or Modify System Process
techniques.description: Adversaries may create or modify system-level processes to repeatedly execute malicious payloads.

techniques.name: Event Triggered Execution
techniques.description: Adversaries may establish persistence or elevate privileges using system mechanisms that trigger execution based on specific events.

techniques.name: Hijack Execution Flow
techniques.description: Adversaries may execute their own malicious payloads by hijacking the way operating systems run programs.

sub_techniques.name: Path Interception by PATH Environment Variable
Path Interception by Search Order Hijacking

techniques.name: Implant Internal Image
techniques.description: Adversaries may implant cloud or container images with malicious code to establish persistence.

techniques.name: Modify Authentication Process
techniques.description: Adversaries may modify authentication mechanisms and processes to access user credentials or enable unauthorized access.

techniques.name: Office Application Startup
techniques.description: Adversaries may leverage Microsoft Office-based applications for persistence between startups.

techniques.name: Pre-OS Boot
techniques.description: Adversaries may abuse pre-OS boot mechanisms as a way to establish persistence on a system.

techniques.name: Server Software Component
techniques.description: Adversaries may abuse legitimate extensible development features of servers to establish persistent access to systems.

techniques.name: Traffic Signaling
techniques.tactic: Persistence, Defense Evasion, Command and Control
techniques.description: Adversaries may use traffic signaling to hide open ports used for persistence or C2.

techniques.name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism
techniques.description: Adversaries may circumvent mechanisms designed to control elevate privileges to gain higher-level permissions.

techniques.name: Access Token Manipulation
techniques.description: Adversaries may modify access tokens to operate under a different user or system security context.

techniques.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
techniques.description: Adversaries may exploit software vulnerabilities in an attempt to elevate privileges.

techniques.name: Domain or Tenant Policy Modification
techniques.description: Adversaries may modify the configuration settings of a domain or identity tenant to evade defenses or escalate privileges.

techniques.name: Process Injection
techniques.description: Adversaries may inject code into processes in order to evade process-based defenses as well as possibly elevate privileges.

techniques.name: Deobfuscate/Decode Files or Information
techniques.description: Adversaries may use obfuscated files or information to hide artifacts of an intrusion from analysis.

techniques.name: Direct Volume Access
techniques.description: Adversaries may directly access a volume to bypass file access controls and file system monitoring.

techniques.name: Execution Guardrails
techniques.description: Adversaries may use execution guardrails to constrain execution or actions based on adversary supplied and environment specific conditions.

techniques.name: Exploitation for Defense Evasion
techniques.description: Adversaries may exploit a system or application vulnerability to bypass security features.

techniques.name: File and Directory Permissions Modification
techniques.description: Adversaries may modify file or directory permissions to evade access control lists and access protected files.

sub_techniques.name: Windows File and Directory Permissions Modification
Linux and Mac File and Directory Permissions Modification

techniques.name: Hide Artifacts
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to hide artifacts associated with their behaviors to evade detection.

techniques.name: Impair Defenses
techniques.description: Adversaries may maliciously modify components of a victim environment in order to hinder or disable defensive mechanisms.

sub_techniques.name: Disable or Modify Linux Audit System

techniques.name: Indicator Removal
techniques.description: Adversaries may delete or modify artifacts generated within systems to remove evidence of their presence.

sub_techniques.name: Clear Linux or Mac System Logs

techniques.name: Masquerading
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to manipulate features of their artifacts to make them appear legitimate.

techniques.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to make an executable or file difficult to discover or analyze by encrypting, encoding, or otherwise obfuscating its contents.

techniques.name: System Binary Proxy Execution
techniques.description: Adversaries may bypass process or signature-based defenses by proxying execution of malicious content with signed or trusted binaries.

techniques.name: Subvert Trust Controls
techniques.description: Adversaries may undermine security controls that will either warn users of untrusted activity or prevent execution of untrusted programs.

techniques.name: Use Alternate Authentication Material
techniques.description: Adversaries may use alternate authentication material to move laterally and bypass normal authentication.

techniques.name: Virtualization/Sandbox Evasion
techniques.description: Adversaries may employ various means to detect and avoid virtualization and analysis environments.

techniques.name: Brute Force
techniques.description: Adversaries may use brute force techniques to gain access to accounts when passwords are unknown or when password hashes are obtained.

techniques.name: Credentials from Password Stores
techniques.description: Adversaries may search for common password storage locations to obtain user credentials.

techniques.name: Exploitation for Credential Access
techniques.description: Adversaries may exploit software vulnerabilities in an attempt to collect credentials.

techniques.name: Forced Authentication
techniques.description: Adversaries may gather credential material by invoking or forcing a user to automatically provide authentication information.

techniques.name: Forge Web Credentials
techniques.description: Adversaries may forge credential materials that can be used to gain access to web applications or Internet services.

techniques.name: Input Capture
techniques.description: Adversaries may use methods of capturing user input to obtain credentials or collect information.

techniques.name: Adversary-in-the-Middle
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to position themselves between two or more networked devices to support follow-on behaviors such as credential access.

techniques.name: OS Credential Dumping
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to dump credentials to obtain account login and credential material from the operating system and software.

techniques.name: Steal Application Access Token
techniques.description: Adversaries can steal application access tokens as a means of acquiring credentials to access remote systems and resources.

techniques.name: Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to subvert Kerberos authentication by stealing or forging Kerberos tickets.

techniques.name: Steal Web Session Cookie
techniques.description: An adversary may steal web application or service session cookies and use them to gain access.

techniques.name: Unsecured Credentials
techniques.description: Adversaries may search compromised systems to find and obtain insecurely stored credentials.

techniques.name: Account Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of valid accounts, usernames, or email addresses on a system or within a compromised environment.

techniques.name: Browser Information Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may enumerate information about browsers to learn more about compromised environments.

techniques.name: Cloud Infrastructure Discovery
techniques.description: An adversary may attempt to discover infrastructure and resources available within an infrastructure-as-a-service environment.

techniques.name: Cloud Service Dashboard
techniques.description: An adversary may use a cloud service dashboard GUI with stolen credentials to gain useful information from an operational cloud environment.

techniques.name: Cloud Service Discovery
techniques.description: An adversary may attempt to enumerate the cloud services running on a system after gaining access.

techniques.name: Domain Trust Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to gather information on domain trust relationships that may be used to identify lateral movement opportunities.

techniques.name: File and Directory Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may enumerate files and directories or may search in specific locations of a host for certain information.

techniques.name: Network Service Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of services running on remote hosts and local network infrastructure devices.

techniques.name: Network Share Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may look for folders and drives shared on remote systems as a means of identifying sources of information to gather.

techniques.name: Network Sniffing
techniques.description: Adversaries may passively sniff network traffic to capture information about an environment.

techniques.name: Password Policy Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to access detailed information about the password policy used within an enterprise network.

techniques.name: Peripheral Device Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to gather information about attached peripheral devices and components connected to a computer system.

techniques.name: Permission Groups Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to discover group and permission settings.

techniques.name: Process Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to get information about running processes on a system.

techniques.name: Query Registry
techniques.description: Adversaries may interact with the Windows Registry to gather information about the system, configuration, and installed software.

techniques.name: Remote System Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of other systems by IP address, hostname, or other logical identifier on a network.

techniques.name: Software Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of software and software versions that are installed on a system.

techniques.name: System Information Discovery
techniques.description: An adversary may attempt to get detailed information about the operating system and hardware.

techniques.name: System Network Configuration Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may look for details about the network configuration and settings of systems they access.

techniques.name: System Network Connections Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of network connections to or from the compromised system.

techniques.name: System Owner/User Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to identify the primary user, currently logged in user, or set of users that commonly use a system.

techniques.name: System Service Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may try to gather information about registered local system services.

techniques.name: Exploitation of Remote Services
techniques.description: Adversaries may exploit remote services to gain unauthorized access to internal systems.

techniques.name: Internal Spearphishing
techniques.description: Adversaries may use internal spearphishing to gain access to additional information or exploit other users within the same organization.

techniques.name: Lateral Tool Transfer
techniques.description: Adversaries may transfer tools or other files between systems in a compromised environment.

techniques.name: Remote Service Session Hijacking
techniques.description: Adversaries may take control of preexisting sessions with remote services to move laterally in an environment.

techniques.name: Remote Services
techniques.description: Adversaries may use valid accounts to log into a service specifically designed to accept remote connections.

techniques.name: Taint Shared Content
techniques.description: Adversaries may deliver payloads to remote systems by adding content to shared storage locations.

techniques.name: Archive Collected Data
techniques.description: An adversary may compress or encrypt data that is collected prior to exfiltration.

techniques.name: Audio Capture
techniques.description: An adversary can leverage a computer peripheral or software to capture audio recordings.

techniques.name: Automated Collection
techniques.description: Once established within a system, an adversary may use automated techniques for collecting internal data.

techniques.name: Browser Session Hijacking
techniques.description: Adversaries may take advantage of security vulnerabilities and inherent functionality in browser software to change content or modify behavior.

techniques.name: Clipboard Data
techniques.description: Adversaries may collect data stored in the clipboard from users copying information within or between applications.

techniques.name: Data from Cloud Storage
techniques.description: Adversaries may access data from cloud storage.

techniques.name: Data from Configuration Repository
techniques.description: Adversaries may collect data related to managed devices from configuration repositories.

techniques.name: Data from Information Repositories
techniques.description: Adversaries may leverage information repositories to mine valuable information.

techniques.name: Data from Local System
techniques.description: Adversaries may search local system sources such as file systems and configuration files or local databases for files of interest.

techniques.name: Data from Network Shared Drive
techniques.description: Adversaries may search network shares on computers they have compromised to find files of interest.

techniques.name: Data from Removable Media
techniques.description: Adversaries may search connected removable media on computers they have compromised to find files of interest.

techniques.name: Email Collection
techniques.description: Adversaries may target user email to collect sensitive information.

techniques.name: Screen Capture
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to take screen captures of the desktop to gather information over the course of an operation.

techniques.name: Video Capture
techniques.description: An adversary can leverage a computer peripheral or software to capture video recordings.

techniques.name: Application Layer Protocol
techniques.description: Adversaries may communicate using OSI application layer protocols to avoid detection by blending in with existing traffic.

techniques.name: Data Encoding
techniques.description: Adversaries may encode data to make the content of command and control traffic more difficult to detect.

techniques.name: Data Obfuscation
techniques.description: Adversaries may obfuscate command and control traffic to make it more difficult to detect.

techniques.name: Dynamic Resolution
techniques.description: Adversaries may dynamically establish connections to command and control infrastructure to evade common detections and remediations.

techniques.name: Encrypted Channel
techniques.description: Adversaries may employ an encryption algorithm to conceal command and control traffic.

techniques.name: Fallback Channels
techniques.description: Adversaries may use fallback or alternate communication channels if the primary channel is compromised.

techniques.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
techniques.description: Adversaries may transfer tools or other files from an external system into a compromised environment.

techniques.name: Multi-Stage Channels
techniques.description: Adversaries may create multiple stages for command and control that are employed under different conditions.

techniques.name: Non-Application Layer Protocol
techniques.description: Adversaries may use an OSI non-application layer protocol for communication between host and C2 server.

techniques.name: Non-Standard Port
techniques.description: Adversaries may communicate using a protocol and port pairing that are typically not associated.

techniques.name: Protocol Tunneling
techniques.description: Adversaries may tunnel network communications to and from a victim system within a separate protocol to avoid detection.

techniques.name: Proxy
techniques.description: Adversaries may use a connection proxy to direct network traffic between systems or act as an intermediary.

techniques.name: Remote Access Software
techniques.description: An adversary may use legitimate desktop support and remote access software to establish an interactive command and control channel.

techniques.name: Web Service
techniques.description: Adversaries may use an existing, legitimate external Web service as a means for relaying data to and from a compromised system.

techniques.name: Automated Exfiltration
techniques.description: Adversaries may exfiltrate data, such as sensitive documents, through the use of automated processing after being gathered during Collection.

techniques.name: Data Transfer Size Limits
techniques.description: An adversary may exfiltrate data in fixed size chunks instead of whole files.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
techniques.description: Adversaries may steal data by exfiltrating it over a different protocol than that of the existing command and control channel.

sub_techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Symmetric Encrypted Non-C2 Protocol
Exfiltration Over Asymmetric Encrypted Non-C2 Protocol

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
techniques.description: Adversaries may steal data by exfiltrating it over an existing command and control channel.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Other Network Medium
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to exfiltrate data over a different network medium than the command and control channel.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Physical Medium
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to exfiltrate data via a physical medium such as a removable drive.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
techniques.description: Adversaries may use an existing, legitimate external Web service to exfiltrate data.

techniques.name: Scheduled Transfer
techniques.description: Adversaries may schedule data exfiltration to be performed only at certain times of day or at certain intervals.

techniques.name: Transfer Data to Cloud Account
techniques.description: Adversaries may exfiltrate data by transferring the data to another cloud account they control.

techniques.name: Account Access Removal
techniques.description: Adversaries may interrupt availability of system and network resources by inhibiting access to accounts utilized by legitimate users.

techniques.name: Data Destruction
techniques.description: Adversaries may destroy data and files on specific systems or in large numbers on a network to interrupt availability.

techniques.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
techniques.description: Adversaries may encrypt data on target systems or on large numbers of systems to interrupt availability.

techniques.name: Data Manipulation
techniques.description: Adversaries may insert, delete, or manipulate data in order to influence external outcomes or hide activity.

techniques.name: Defacement
techniques.description: Adversaries may modify visual content available internally or externally to an enterprise network.

techniques.name: Disk Wipe
techniques.description: Adversaries may wipe or corrupt raw disk data on specific systems or in large numbers to interrupt availability.

techniques.name: Endpoint Denial of Service
techniques.description: Adversaries may perform Endpoint Denial of Service attacks to degrade or block availability of services to users.

techniques.name: Financial Theft
techniques.description: Adversaries may steal monetary resources from targets through extortion, social engineering, technical theft, or other methods.

techniques.name: Firmware Corruption
techniques.description: Adversaries may overwrite or corrupt the flash memory contents of system BIOS or other firmware.

techniques.name: Inhibit System Recovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may delete or remove built-in data and turn off services designed to aid in the recovery of a corrupted system.

techniques.name: Network Denial of Service
techniques.description: Adversaries may perform Network Denial of Service attacks to degrade or block availability of targeted resources to users.

techniques.name: Resource Hijacking
techniques.description: Adversaries may leverage the resources of co-opted systems to complete resource-intensive tasks.

techniques.name: Service Stop
techniques.description: Adversaries may stop or disable services on a system to render those services unavailable to legitimate users.

techniques.name: System Shutdown/Reboot
techniques.description: Adversaries may shutdown or reboot systems to interrupt access to or aid in the destruction of those systems.

techniques.name: Active Scanning
techniques.description: Adversaries may execute active reconnaissance scans to gather information that can be used during targeting.

techniques.name: Gather Victim Host Information
techniques.description: Adversaries may gather information about the victim's hosts that can be used during targeting.

techniques.name: Gather Victim Identity Information
techniques.description: Adversaries may gather information about the victim's identity that can be used during targeting.

techniques.name: Gather Victim Network Information
techniques.description: Adversaries may gather information about the victim's networks that can be used during targeting.

techniques.name: Gather Victim Org Information
techniques.description: Adversaries may gather information about the victim's organization that can be used during targeting.

techniques.name: Phishing for Information
techniques.description: Adversaries may send phishing messages to elicit sensitive information that can be used during targeting.

techniques.name: Search Closed Sources
techniques.description: Adversaries may search and gather information about victims from closed sources that can be used during targeting.

techniques.name: Search Open Technical Databases
techniques.description: Adversaries may search freely available technical databases for information about victims that can be used during targeting.

techniques.name: Search Open Websites/Domains
techniques.description: Adversaries may search freely available websites for information about victims that can be used during targeting.

techniques.name: Search Victim-Owned Websites
techniques.description: Adversaries may search websites owned by the victim for information that can be used during targeting.

techniques.name: Acquire Infrastructure
techniques.description: Adversaries may buy, lease, rent, or obtain infrastructure that can be used during targeting.

techniques.name: Compromise Accounts
techniques.description: Adversaries may compromise accounts with services that can be used during targeting.

techniques.name: Compromise Infrastructure
techniques.description: Adversaries may compromise third-party infrastructure that can be used during targeting.

techniques.name: Develop Capabilities
techniques.description: Adversaries may build capabilities that can be used during targeting.

techniques.name: Establish Accounts
techniques.description: Adversaries may create and cultivate accounts with services that can be used during targeting.

techniques.name: Obtain Capabilities
techniques.description: Adversaries may buy, steal, or download capabilities that can be used during targeting.

techniques.name: Stage Capabilities
techniques.description: Adversaries may upload, install, or otherwise set up capabilities that can be used during targeting.
